assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as a cert-manager webhook compatibility issue after control plane upgrade and explains that the webhook is preventing pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides both commands: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides multiple immediate fix options including patching failurePolicy to Ignore and deleting the webhook configurations entirely

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes specific commands: 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Labels the deletion as 'nuclear option', emphasizes backing up first, and clearly categorizes these as temporary fixes that need proper resolution

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes specific section on checking cert-manager version compatibility with commands and mentions that cert-manager 1.13+ supports Kubernetes 1.28-1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides detailed helm upgrade commands and specifies upgrading to cert-manager 1.14.x for K8s 1.32 compatibility as the permanent solution

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option 2 specifically shows patching the failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as a safer alternative to deletion, presented before the deletion option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Multiple verification commands provided: 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --dry-run=server', 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it', and a full deployment test in the validation section
